warnings: This index confirms the sanitary discipline set for house #3 (2-story) runs SN-01..SN-10 (10 sheets), matching pages 46-55 one-to-one (SN-01=png46 ... SN-10=png55). This is 2 more sheets than house #2's 8-sheet set (SN-01..SN-08) and 3 more than house #1's 7-sheet set.

The two extra sheets vs. house #2 are: SN-04 'ไดอะแกรมระบบสุขาภิบาลแนวดิ่ง' (vertical sanitary riser diagram, new) and a SPLIT floor plan into SN-05 'แปลนระบบสุขาภิบาลพื้นชั้นล่าง' (ground floor) + SN-06 'แปลนระบบสุขาภิบาลพื้นชั้นบน' (upper floor) where house #2 (1-story) had only one combined floor-plan sheet (its SN-04). This confirms the 2-story layout produces two separate sanitary floor plans as anticipated by the task brief. SN-07 (bathroom detail expansion) then shifts down one slot relative to house #2's SN-04 combined sheet, and there is no separate roof-drainage plan sheet analogous to house #2's SN-05 -- roof drainage for this house appears to be folded into the riser diagram (SN-04) and/or floor plans instead; to be confirmed on those pages.
Page also contains two other tables (pipe type schedule, symbol legend) captured in sibling files.

Read directly from this page's own image (not copied from house #1 or #2). Content is near-identical boilerplate to house #1/#2's equivalent index page apart from the drawing number (SN-58080 vs SN-58078/SN-58079), plan_type label ('บ้านครอบครัวไทยร่วมสมัย 3' vs '...1'/'...2'), and the 10-row sheet list reflecting the 2-story-specific sheets described above -- confirms all three houses share the same standard sanitary drawing-set template from the same design office, scaled to each house's story count.
